entries.title: Apple Pay Address Collection
entries.description: Apple Pay payment sheet can now collect billing and shipping addresses when configured via `required_fields`. Default behavior is unchanged when not configured.

entries.title: Apple Pay BIN Available Pre-Payment
entries.description: DPAN BIN is now available in the OTT before payment, enabling BIN-based promotions and discounts. Existing card BIN flow is unaffected.

entries.title: Improved Apple Pay Availability Detection
entries.description: Apple Pay button now appears more accurately on supported devices, reducing cases where the button shows for users who cannot complete an Apple Pay payment.

entries.title: Google Pay Address Collection
entries.description: Google Pay payment sheet can now collect billing address, shipping address, and cardholder name when configured via `required_fields`. Address detail level adjusts automatically based on what is requested.

entries.title: Google Pay Contact Field Collection
entries.description: Google Pay payment sheet can now collect customer email and phone number when configured via `contactFields`. Works in both standard and seamless external-button integrations.

entries.title: Separate Billing and Shipping Sections
entries.description: Card form now renders billing and shipping as distinct sections with their own headers and an optional `Address line 2` field. When both addresses are required, a `Billing address is the same as shipping` checkbox appears, enabled by default.

entries.title: Compact Card Form Layout
entries.description: New slimmer card form layout with tighter spacing, grouped card details, and inline field-level error messages. Available across all SDK form variants. Behind a feature flag for A/B testing.

entries.title: Card Form Auto-Advance
entries.description: Card form auto-advances focus once fields reach their expected length (PAN by detected scheme, expiry, CVV), reducing the number of taps to complete the form. Manual selection always takes precedence. Behind a feature flag, default off, for A/B testing.

entries.title: Real-Time Card Field Validation
entries.description: Card form fields now validate on blur once the user has interacted with them, instead of only on Pay click. Errors clear immediately when the user corrects an invalid field.

entries.title: Dynamic Enrollment Actions
entries.description: Enrollment flow now supports server-driven dynamic UI components: image, OTP, PIN, and info screens, rendered based on the fields returned by the server.

entries.title: Smarter Currency Display
entries.description: Currency now shows a symbol only for the 21 currencies with globally unique symbols (€, £, ₹, R$, ₩, ₺, ₪, etc.). All others display the 3-letter ISO code (for example `COP 9.200.000`, `MXN 1.500,00`). USD is the only currency that owns the `$` symbol.

entries.title: Hide Yuno Secure Payment Badge
entries.description: The `Secure Payment with Yuno` badge can now be hidden via Checkout Builder. When disabled, the badge is fully removed from the DOM in both the standard checkout and the Click to Pay flow.

entries.title: EBANX Device Session Reliability
entries.description: Improved reliability of device ID propagation in payment requests, fixing Payment Link flows where it was occasionally dropped before reaching EBANX.

entries.title: Improved EBANX Device Session Handling
entries.description: Improved EBANX device session recovery in supported fraud flows.

entries.title: POST Redirect Support
entries.description: Added support for payment providers that require `POST` redirects.

entries.title: Expanded Required Field Support
entries.description: Added support for additional required payment data such as `shipping_address` in supported flows.

entries.title: Improved Session Handling
entries.description: Improved Secure Fields behavior in session-based payment flows.

entries.title: Improved Card Retry Flows
entries.description: Enhanced retry behavior for card payments in supported checkout flows.

entries.title: More Reliable Payment Status Updates
entries.description: Improved payment status handling when real-time connection tracking is interrupted.

entries.title: Improved QR, Barcode, and Image Rendering
entries.description: Improved rendering for payment steps that rely on images, QR codes, or barcodes.

entries.title: Improved OTP and PIN Flows
entries.description: Refined OTP and PIN-based payment steps for a smoother authentication experience.

entries.title: Improved Button Rendering
entries.description: Improved Google Pay button rendering for a more consistent checkout experience.

entries.title: Card Type Filtering Support
entries.description: Added support for card type restrictions in Click to Pay flows.

entries.title: Transaction Amount Support for Passkey Flows
entries.description: Click to Pay initialization now includes transaction amount metadata for supported passkey flows.

entries.title: unMountSdk() Helper
entries.description: Introduced a new top-level `unMountSdk()` method for explicit SDK cleanup. Call it when removing the SDK from the page to avoid memory leaks.

entries.title: Rollback Cancel Flow
entries.description: Unified cancel flow events. The SDK now explicitly reports `CANCELED_BY_USER` in the `yunoPaymentResult` callback for all cancellation scenarios.

entries.title: Legacy Event Rollback
entries.description: Legacy cancel-related events have been consolidated into the standard result flow. No new API changes required.

entries.title: Forter Token Listener
entries.description: Added a listener for the `ftr:tokenReady` event to ensure reliable capture of the Forter session token. Previously the token could be missed on slow page loads.

entries.title: Metadata Support
entries.description: The cancel flow now includes a `metadata` parameter with a `paymentCreated` boolean, letting merchants determine whether a payment object was created before cancellation.

entries.title: Contact Info Passthrough
entries.description: The SDK now automatically collects and forwards the customer's email, phone, and name from `shippingContact`. No configuration required.

entries.title: No OTT Flow
entries.description: Added support for a `REDIRECT` workflow that skips OTT creation, enabling PayPal payments without a prior server-side session.

entries.title: PayPal Button Modal
entries.description: New `PaypalButtonModal` component that renders the PayPal button inside a modal overlay, useful for merchants using a custom checkout UI.

entries.title: Subresource Integrity Support
entries.description: The SDK script tag now supports the `integrity` attribute for SRI-compliant loading, preventing unauthorized code injection. The `@yuno-payments/sdk-web` npm package exposes a `loadScript` method for SRI-compliant dynamic loading.

entries.title: Arabic Language and RTL Layout
entries.description: Added Arabic (`ar`) language support. The checkout UI automatically switches to a Right-to-Left layout when Arabic is selected — no extra configuration required.

example.title: Initialize with Arabic

entries.title: Faster Initialization
entries.description: Improved initialization performance for both Lite and Full SDKs, reducing time-to-interactive on first load.

entries.title: Detailed Error Codes
entries.description: Configuration errors now surface more descriptive error codes, making it easier to diagnose integration issues during development.

entries.title: 3DS Modal Centering
entries.description: Fixed an issue where the 3DS challenge modal was not centered on mobile devices. The modal now correctly fills and centers within the viewport.

entries.title: Backend-Driven Warning Banner
entries.description: Flexible payment-action screens (e.g. Punto Pago) can now render a server-driven banner (title + description) after the instruction steps. Configured server-side; no merchant code change required.

entries.title: mountExternalButtons Method
entries.description: New `mountExternalButtons(buttons)` method lets merchants render Google Pay and Apple Pay buttons in any custom location within their UI, providing full control over button placement and styling.

example.title: Mount wallet buttons in custom locations

entries.title: Button Unmounting Methods
entries.description: Added `unmountExternalButton(paymentMethodType)` to remove a single external wallet button and `unmountAllExternalButtons()` to remove all mounted wallet buttons at once.

example.title: Unmount wallet buttons

entries.title: Wallet Buttons as Direct Buttons
entries.description: Google Pay and Apple Pay now appear as direct action buttons instead of radio-button list items, providing a cleaner and more prominent placement in the checkout UI.

entries.title: PayPal Enrollment UX
entries.description: The PayPal payment sheet now opens immediately after the customer selects PayPal for enrollment or payment, removing an extra confirmation step for a faster checkout experience.

entries.title: Lite SDK External Buttons Required
entries.description: Google Pay and Apple Pay in the Lite Web SDK now require explicit mounting via `mountExternalButtons()`. Previously they rendered automatically as radio buttons. Lite SDK integrations must add a `mountExternalButtons` call — Full SDK integrations are not affected.

migration_notes.before:
// Lite SDK — buttons rendered automatically
yuno.startCheckout({ ... })

entries.title: PayPal Installments Enrollment
entries.description: Customers can now enroll in PayPal installment plans directly through the PayPal payment flow, providing flexible payment options at checkout.

entries.title: PayPal External Button Redirect
entries.description: PayPal can now be integrated via redirect flow with external buttons, giving merchants greater flexibility in building their payment UI.

entries.title: Card Payment Voucher Messages
entries.description: Added voucher messaging for card payments, giving customers clear confirmation and transaction details immediately after a successful payment.

entries.title: Language Override for Loader
entries.description: The Yuno loader now accepts a `language` property, allowing explicit UI language control independent of browser settings. Useful for multi-language environments.

entries.title: Enhanced Click-to-Pay Rendering
entries.description: Improved rendering support for Click-to-Pay (C2P) elements, streamlining the checkout experience for enrolled cards.

entries.title: Smart Payment Method Grouping
entries.description: When a customer has an enrolled payment method, all other available methods are grouped under a collapsible "More options" dropdown. The enrolled method is prominently displayed by default.

entries.title: European and Asian Language Support
entries.description: Added support for German (`de`), Dutch (`nl`), Swedish (`sv`), French (`fr`), Italian (`it`), Japanese (`ja`), and Korean (`ko`). Pass the corresponding code via the `language` initialization parameter.

entries.title: General Bug Fixes
entries.description: Resolved various stability issues to improve reliability across payment flows.

entries.title: Enhanced Styling and Branding
entries.description: Improved visual consistency and expanded customization options across checkout components.

entries.title: Optional Initialization Options
entries.description: Added an optional `options` parameter to `Yuno.initialize` for teams with advanced use cases such as custom session handling or tracking requirements.

entries.title: Extended continuePayment Parameters
entries.description: `continuePayment` now accepts `checkoutSession`, `showPaymentStatus`, `yunoPaymentResult`, `yunoError`, `countryCode`, and `language`, allowing configuration overrides during payment continuation without restarting the checkout.

example.title: continuePayment with overrides

entries.title: 21+ Language Support
entries.description: Expanded language coverage to over 21 locales including Chinese Simplified (`zh-CN`), Chinese Traditional (`zh-TW`), Vietnamese (`vi`), Russian (`ru`), Turkish (`tr`), Polish (`pl`), and more. Pass the language code to the `language` parameter in `startCheckout`.

entries.title: Lite SDK continuePayment Overrides
entries.description: The Lite SDK `continuePayment` method now accepts the same configuration overrides available in the Full SDK, enabling dynamic session, language, and callback changes during payment continuation.

example.title: Lite SDK continuePayment with overrides

entries.title: Lite SDK Expanded Language Support
entries.description: Lite SDK v1.2 adds support for 18+ locales (up from 7 in v1.1), including European and Asian language codes such as `de`, `fr`, `it`, `ja`, `ko`, `ru`, and `zh-CN`.

entries.title: Async SDK Methods
entries.description: The `initialize()`, `mountCheckout()`, and `startCheckout()` methods now return Promises. Add `await` or `.then()` to each call — synchronous usage will no longer work.

migration_notes.after:
await yuno.initialize(publicApiKey, config)
await yuno.mountCheckout({ ... })
await yuno.startCheckout({ ... })

example.title: Async initialization (v1.1)
example.snippet:
const yuno = await Yuno.initialize(PUBLIC_API_KEY);
await yuno.startCheckout(config);

entries.title: continuePayment Method
entries.description: Introduced `continuePayment()` for handling complex payment flows. Call it when the API response includes `sdk_action_required: true` — the SDK then automatically renders 3DS challenges, external wallet steps, or redirects as needed.

example.title: Handle sdk_action_required with continuePayment

entries.title: Simplified 3DS Integration
entries.description: 3DS data collection and setup are now bundled into the payment creation step. Remove any standalone 3DS setup service calls — implement `continuePayment()` and `yunoPaymentResult()` callbacks instead.

entries.title: Boleto Bancário Support
entries.description: Boleto Bancário is now available for merchants operating in Brazil, expanding local payment options for customers who prefer offline payment methods.

entries.title: Hybrid Cards Default to Credit
entries.description: Brazilian hybrid cards are now processed as credit by default, improving authorization rates for Brazil-based merchants.

entries.title: Airwallex Security Integration
entries.description: Integrated Airwallex as an additional security layer for web payments, enhancing fraud protection without requiring extra configuration.

entries.title: Forter Fraud Prevention
entries.description: Integrated Forter's fraud prevention technology. The SDK automatically collects device signals; no separate setup is required.

entries.title: Checkout.com 3DS Support
entries.description: Added support for Checkout.com as a 3DS provider. The SDK renders the authentication page automatically when the transaction provider is `checkout3ds`.

entries.title: Unlimit 3DS Support
entries.description: Introduced 3DS authentication support for Unlimit payments, enhancing fraud prevention for merchants using the Unlimit provider.

entries.title: Click-to-Pay Enhancements
entries.description: Terms & Conditions and card logos now update dynamically based on the selected card. C2P options are hidden for unsupported cards. A phone number field has been added for C2P registration. Compliance settings for `privacy` and `tnc` are forwarded with each card.

entries.title: Inline Card Input
entries.description: Customers can now enter card details directly beneath the Card option without navigating to a separate screen. Fields persist when switching payment methods, and the flow is fully compatible with installments and the card selector.

entries.title: Lite SDK Initial Release
entries.description: Launched the Lite Web SDK (v1.1) — a streamlined integration offering essential payment methods, async `initialize()` support, and core callbacks (`yunoPaymentResult`, `yunoError`) with reduced setup complexity.

example.title: Lite SDK basic checkout

entries.title: Initial SDK Release
entries.description: First stable release of the Yuno Web SDK. Provides four integration variants: Full Checkout SDK, Lite Checkout SDK, Seamless SDK, and Headless SDK — covering a full range of UI customization needs.

entries.title: Core Payment Methods
entries.description: Out-of-the-box support for credit and debit cards, digital wallets (PayPal, Apple Pay, Google Pay), bank transfers, direct debits, and regional local payment methods across multiple currencies and countries.

entries.title: Three Integration Modes
entries.description: SDK can be loaded via an HTML `<script>` tag, dynamic JavaScript, or as an npm module (`@yuno-payments/sdk-web`), supporting both classic and modern JavaScript framework workflows.

entries.title: Seven Language Support
entries.description: Initial international support includes Spanish (`es`), English (`en`), Portuguese (`pt`), Filipino (`fil`), Indonesian (`id`), Malay (`ms`), and Thai (`th`).

entries.title: Core SDK Methods
entries.description: Provides `Yuno.initialize()`, `startCheckout()`, `mountCheckout()`, and `startPayment()` as the foundational API surface. All methods are synchronous in v1.0.

example.title: Full checkout initialization (v1.0)
